Patch release. One user-visible bug fix surfaced from the first external-tester `/setup-claude-wiki` run. No new features.

## Tier-1 fix

- **`setup-claude-wiki` closing-hint flattening** — the closing message had `/add-page`, `/lint-vault`, `/query-wiki`, and `/daily-log` packed into a single dense line with `(/daily-log` is optional, planned for v0.1.1+`)` as a trailing parenthetical. The model paraphrasing the message at session-end consistently dropped that nuance and announced **all four** skills as "future / not-yet-shipped." Test users finished setup believing only `setup-claude-wiki` was available today.

  Fixed by restructuring into two explicitly-labelled bullet groups so the contrast survives any reasonable paraphrase:

  - **Available now** — `/add-page`, `/lint-vault`, `/query-wiki` (with the kepano + Obsidian-running prerequisites)
  - **Planned in upcoming plugin updates** — `/daily-log` (v0.1.1+, optional)

  Applied to both JP and EN closing messages (`SKILL.md` step 8 + the mirrored bodies in `references/templates.md`).

## Verification

- `grep -nE "today.*available|今すぐ使える"` confirms contrast headers exist in all four locations
- The dense single-line variant is no longer present in `setup-claude-wiki/`
- v0.1.0 / v0.1.0.1 / v0.1.1 closing-hint text was the same dense form across all three releases — first surfaced as a bug only when the model output was inspected on a real test-user run

## Why this slipped through three releases

The text was technically correct (the parenthetical did say "v0.1.1+ optional") so static review didn't flag it. The failure mode was an **LLM-paraphrasing instability** — only visible when the closing message renders end-to-end through the model. Lesson for future releases: closing/summary messages need a **structural** contrast (separated bullets, headers) not a parenthetical contrast, because parentheticals get dropped first when models compress.
